title: Patriots reportedly cut linebacker Jahlani Tavai
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The New England Patriots released linebacker Jahlani Tavai after five seasons with the team.
Tavai had over 110 tackles in 2023 and 2024 but played only 22% of defensive snaps in 2025.
He battled injuries and a rare autoimmune disease, impacting his 2025 performance, and played 12 games this season.

title: RFK Jr. blasted Dunkin’ for selling sugary drinks. Which menu items fit his criteria?
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Health Secretary RFK Jr. criticized Dunkin' for selling drinks with high sugar content, targeting items like large frozen coffees.
Dunkin's Butter Pecan Swirl and Caramel Creme Frozen Coffees contain up to 172 grams of sugar, among the highest on the menu.
Forty drinks at Dunkin' meet RFK Jr.'s 115 grams of sugar benchmark, all being 'frozen' or blended beverages.

title: Two Mass. lottery players win big on new scratch ticket games
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Jose Fernandes of Woburn won $2.6 million from the 'Cash Doubler' ticket, purchased in Melrose.
Yancy Contreras Menjivar of Nantucket won $1.3 million from the 'Stacked' ticket, purchased at Old South Diner.
The Melrose gas station and Old South Diner will receive bonuses of $40,000 and $20,000, respectively.

title: ‘Goals are worth chasing’: Why this single mom is running the 2026 Boston Marathon
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Kimberly Long, 42, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon inspired by her growth in running since the COVID pandemic.
As a single mom, Long aims to demonstrate the value of perseverance and goal-setting to her son through her marathon journey.
Long is part of the Dana-Farber Marathon Challenge team, raising funds for cancer research in honor of a friend's daughter.

title: PHOTOS: This was the scene at Sunday’s Shamrock Splash into Boston Harbor
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Hundreds participated in the 16th annual Shamrock Splash at Constitution Beach to raise funds for summer beach programs.
The event, hosted by Save the Harbor/Save the Bay and JetBlue, supports the Better Beaches Grant Program.
Last summer, funds supported nearly 200 community events on Department of Conservation and Recreation beaches.

title: ‘We’re not concerned about Boston’: Pistons’ head coach referenced Celtics amid four-game losing streak
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Pistons' head coach J.B. Bickerstaff commented on Boston Celtics amid a four-game losing streak but remained focused on his team.
Despite losing streak, Pistons still lead the Eastern Conference by 2.5 games, with the Celtics gaining momentum.
Pistons to face Nets next, who won the previous matchup, while Celtics prepare for road games against Spurs and Thunder.

title: A Mass. destination makes an excellent Memorial Day weekend trip, according to Conde Nast Traveler
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Cape Cod was named a top Memorial Day weekend destination by Conde Nast Traveler for 2026.
Known for whale watching, Cape Cod offers tours with Hyannis and Sea Salt Charters following safe practices.
Recommended accommodations include The Villages at Ocean Edge Resort in Brewster and Chatham Bars Inn in Chatham.

title: Boston’s Patricia Cornwell sees her books land on screen with ‘Scarpetta’
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Patricia Cornwell's 'Scarpetta' novels are adapted into a series starring Nicole Kidman, premiering March 11 on Prime Video.
The series is developed by Liz Sarnoff and executive produced by Jamie Lee Curtis, who also stars as Dorothy.
Cornwell's candid memoir 'True Crime' details her challenging past and is scheduled for release on May 2.

title: Patriots free agency live updates: Alec Pierce re-signs with Colts
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots sign Dre'Mont Jones to a 3-year, $39 million deal for pass-rushing reinforcement.
Colts re-sign Alec Pierce with a 4-year, $116 million contract, reducing Patriots' WR options.
Patriots release LB Jahlani Tavai ahead of NFL free agency legal tampering period.

title: Boston Marathon runner aims to complete marathons in all 50 states
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Zara Harding, 46, from Henderson, Nevada, aims to run marathons in all 50 U.S. states.
Harding has completed over a dozen marathons since her colorectal cancer diagnosis in 2021.
After the Boston Marathon, she will have only 10 states left to complete her goal.
